demographics.living_situation: young child living at home with a parent and siblings

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The sores on my child's face are spreading so fast — they're constantly weeping this yellow fluid and crusting up around the nose and mouth, and it's too painful for them to eat. It's really contagious and I'm scared the other kids will catch it. (Parent calling on their behalf.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's blown up in the last 12 hours from one little red spot under the nose into a big weeping crusty patch, and I want to know how to stop it spreading and get them something for it tonight.

symptom_narrative.onset: started about two days ago as one small red sore just under the nose, right after a mild cold
symptom_narrative.duration: about 48 hours, but it has spread rapidly in the last 12 hours
symptom_narrative.character: golden, honey-coloured crusts on a red base that keep weeping a yellow fluid; the crusts feel tight and pull when they move their mouth
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: much worse and spreading fast; it's sore enough that they don't want to eat
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: mostly around the nose and mouth, and now a couple of new spots have appeared on the chin
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, and it has clearly spread over the last half a day
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really — it keeps weeping and crusting over again
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: moving the mouth pulls the crusts and hurts; scratching seems to spread it to new spots
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: mild itch, and it stings; they keep touching and picking at it. No high fever that I can tell and they're still up and about, just uncomfortable and off their food
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I've had to keep them home from childcare because it's so contagious, and they're eating less because it hurts

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a mild cold / runny nose in the few days beforehand

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: young child in childcare; siblings at home and we've been sharing hand towels in the bathroom, so I'm worried about it spreading in the house

objective_data_offered.value: a patch of thick golden, honey-coloured crusts on bright red skin around the nose and mouth, roughly the size of a large stamp, with clear yellow fluid weeping from it; a couple of smaller matching spots on the chin

parent says the child is uncomfortable and keeps touching the face, winces when the crusts pull on moving the mouth, and is eating less because it is sore, but is otherwise up and about and behaving fairly normally
about 37.5 on the home thermometer — feels a tiny bit warm but no real fever
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: siblings at home and shared hand towels in the bathroom
